## Los Angeles Dodgers Repeat as Champs
Will Smith's 11th-inning home run lifted the Los Angeles Dodgers to a 5-4 win over the Toronto Blue Jays in Game 7, securing their second straight World Series championship. [See the homerun here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2OqzAQxfGngS7IM5iPFC7SpMttb2mBPYbJGoxsK17efsW-wrZHR7_i__-lXxN73pd_00Yqu6B3KslTzhS1DXahmArvlj-8L1vYv-hMkTQK7G4AN4HaTuxPLfWjtsoRIbU1KRh6FCPIO9Srap25t2RgnAaAmWZD8yxxvJu-70aQc83q8gAEAgjZicb2DtCA7OUwDl1nKinIcg6xeQfe80rOn-FDsTFhq71acz5S1T4qfFb4LKU0LnynI8Scrse1TdmsFT7dZm7tOfgDtvZNnT1WiXVU9ijsPad-qKRYton9r5z_GOSj8CcAAP__bH11Rg). Los Angeles rallied from 3-0 and 4-2 deficits, and needed a [9th-inning game tying home run](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFy6yAQgOHToM4edrUSqKDwK1Q9p03JYLHIJEh4gNjx7TPOFdL-xVf872d7djHFfX1zG5sWst35URO3xsX67Fcu9RF3H-9xX7e8f_KzFrYocTgAHCRa72J6WrKnzpvAjNx3bECNKDXQBN3V4EROa9U7XEIgJVH6C_rLANCHZdG6i-blAUgEkDTIox8D4AI0ktJqGBZBkn1suRw_ctzblUN65juX45K3Lplra7cq-pPAWeD8_aoC5_P_fwLn2lz7qgJnmDRpBDUpUEA0KE1dMf72iCnFOipBct1cTL9m--OKu8GfAAAA__-2KG8r) from Miguel Rojas to become [MLB's first repeat champion since](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O3CAUhZ8Gd4wAg38Kim3cbdqUFgvXnpvB3BHc2Jq3jyaKlAfY7khHOjrf9_Nz_QyYsew_wgGeN1oLXC0DM9Q1UdqhtgtLwhPLflB5wKtVWI0yTmotlVlTwPxa7frRJb8BGOg78HocjJq0nXV391Mfp3kI6msLIQzO6XlUAVyc5n4wsxo79O89rZXRWlmnbmnYtInaDnacRueisAoSMtXbL8LCd9jyi06ot0hHl_2d-dlE_yHMIszy4BzehTBLe1LlJszyj-R_kl8QWDJVKkzSSSuxvIGwFCx7kxtV2SBSSTJSaRB_M54gL6o5yQYVoUlGziDM0lWfnhfmjG0YhVX7ETD_vcbfNHp68ycAAP__ob6MiA) the 1998-2000 Yankees and the NL's first since the 1975-76 Reds. Yoshinobu Yamamoto earned the World Series [Most Valuable Player Award](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DvOpDAQBODTQAZyN-YxgYNJyGbTDRHGDfSuHyPbi8XtVzNX-OOSqvTV79fyWtmyP36tjlTew-KpJEs5U1xMMAfFVNgbvtgfLvi_dKdICwrsG4BG4GJWtvcil2dt1E6E1NWkYBxQTCAfUJ8K9fQwGrZNagEw6YnGrhOyR-jlY510zerTByAQQMhetGbYATeQgxynse-3SgoynENs_wT2-aTd3uGi2G7B1VadOb9T1T0rnCucSymts_qTVTh_OBXOd0gn-6D_NffqVhdyaAr71HwhJURrmkSRKTXuetdRmXdhazkNYyXF4Va23638w4suhf8DAAD__2hje7A) after pitching 2 2/3 scoreless innings in Game 7, just 24 hours after starting Game 6. Yamamoto finished the World Series with a 1.02 ERA, 17 2/3 innings, 15 strikeouts, and three wins, becoming the first pitcher to record three victories in a World Series since Arizona's Randy Johnson in 2001.
## Canadian PM Apologizes to Trump
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Saturday he [apologized to President Donald Trump](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMGK3DAQRL9GvmmQ2rK9c_BhkmDIYUPIITmaHqnl6awsGUkzxn8fnF_YW0HBo-r9eZ_fkQPH5QeuNFaf5kh7CVQr5dklt1AuO0fHL47LmuIHHSXTDAo6qbVUMDvkcMxmvjVu9ERAbUOjHnpQb9pcdfMYO2ut0dZQf70DXH2nhzu0yrUD9Z1RXcPjydNagdbKdOrieq_BatOb4W3oOiuMIsc15cvfxLE-yIcjvShfbFqbMD5q3YpobwImAdO-75e1xLMTMFGUzyJgOm8JmLYUuLI9Y83PdZML1SJxSyEth_Q5rdJiRMcY5bZK9JWyxFhZVszsvcyEC0aJTt7RfnjOdLIwy9tN__z6Xf0S7ZQsO9F--3Jqpb38prw1eXTbziFw6Qdh1LIih__z6yetv0b4FwAA___cY5hb) over a political ad that quoted former President Ronald Reagan’s 1987 radio address criticizing trade barriers. The ad infuriated Trump, who [ended trade talks with Canada](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO5CAQRL_Gzjyi2xhMQDDJZHPphRaCxtN3GCzMzsh_v_L-woalkqr03t_n8nScOK9_3Ea2xbJk-hyJWqO6hBJWqseHc-A353Ur-T-dR6UFBU4DwCBwCY7Tucjl3gcbiZDGnixohWIGaaB_WTliINLzDBqNNgKC0gqMU6ikH4Xr2V57AAIBhJzELagI6EEqqWc9Tb6TggK3Um__Cuf2opjO8qZ682Xrk321th_deO_w0eHD7RfCVV2hNvaJOnx4VzOdg3fZBTe0-rXtQ3OVYxxcGGaQkzDKzAadn3AK0hOCkRjHSEaPfbVh_3BKfCjdSbFujtPPffultbfF7wAAAP__zop-gg) and said he plans to hike tariffs on imports of Canadian goods by an extra 10%. Carney said he told Ontario’s premier not to run an anti-tariff ad. Trump also [alleged that Canada was attempting to meddle](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAQRb8Gd0QwAewUFGnc5bWvtGZhbM8uBguzifz3q-wvbH11z5HO_8f0QE6cl3-4kW9zmTK9jkStUZ1iiQvV48U58pPzspX8RedRaQIFVmotFUwROZ2Tme5d9DMR0LUjr3sHatDmprvVOxsGCOgIb6QGa3szDIQfNhhERT127N88rRVorYxVl-hmDUEbZ_qhtzYIoyhyK_XyWTi3leZ0lifVSyhbl_za2n6I613AKGBsK62c0nsTMO4lcTgFjJwb1YyNS8YkYLR2MM71MmDNdMpWv7ddBswYUTasPM8So8S9pLK8_131cX9xSny4Xhi1bMi_kq79sdvTw08AAAD__znOgxA) with an upcoming case before the Supreme Court looking at the challenges to the administration’s tariffs. Asked if he would [resume trade negotiations](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O5CAQBOCnwRkjaOOfCQg2cbaXXmj1QuPpWwwWMDPyPf3J9wobl_Spqn5_rp_IkdP2C3eyLeQ10btGao3K6rPfqNQ3J88vTtue0zedtdAKCgaptVSweuR4rmb96LwNREB9R1ZPI6hZm7vuHtY4mA0FPWrlv6b5Pqo7DOGLlHODmrHv2F6e1gq0VmZQNz8GDU6b0UzzNAxOGEWeWy63P5lTe1CIZ35Rubm8d9E-Wjuq6D8ELAKWdB65tisSsFywgEUrAUuvBSzPKq-FAhaHCT1jkscudyzf0mFJdEo8cswb_yUvQy4SU2PZsHAIshBumCR62cpzP2TF85K6Yv3x5hi5jpMwatuR4_9u7YeXviz8CwAA___HA4rq) with Carney, Trump said, _“No, but I have a very good relationship. I like him a lot.”_
## List: Top U.S. Winter Holiday Destinations
Chicago and Las Vegas were ranked as the [top cold-weather and warm-weather winter holiday destinations](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOqzAQAL_GdETexRhSuEhDl9e-0jLxAntn7Mh2gvj7U-4Xrh5pRpr_d3t3HDiu_9xOpi7JRjpKoFopW5_8SrkcHD2_Oa57it90lkwWJfYtQCvResfhtMreGm8WIqSuIQODRjmCukKzmVFqGrplVgQjQacRO907R7O-9n6ZoWHz8QFIBJCqlxevF8AHKK2Gcej7h1CSPNeUL1-JY91oCWd6U7480t4Es9X6LKK7CZwETocLger2mj9U4ET-JXCaqdT24Fgpt1sK7N3ZeiqVo6ucYhE4DddONtn458EhcNGDUHLdHYffTP3jnbfBnwAAAP__Rph57g), respectively, for the upcoming season in a survey by WalletHub. The rankings reflect the cheapest U.S. destinations that are also the easiest to reach based on two weeks of flight data, safety indicators, and weather predictions. The Old Farmer’s Almanac forecasts****[a temperate and uneventful winter](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO8yAQhJ_G7myxGLBTUKRJl7_9S0S8i7N3GCJA8eXtTzndG1w7M5pP-v5f3dVz5LT98zvZFrJLdNRIrVFxmHGjUg9OyE9O257TJ71qISeF1APAIKRDz_HllDv3aAORpKknC7ORYgF1gv5uTTBAE3o8Ba1R62UKN7l4ozSF5XQzPdv3H4CQAEJpMaIJIFdQRs3LrPXaKUHILZfxI3NqdwrxlZ9UxjXvfbT31h61m86dvHTychzH6OPuk1_f_Tvh1KgM9NUoIeEQcqHV1zYEX3Yqdfid98Xi4-AYuZq5U2LbPccfRvujmqeV3wEAAP__qxR6ag) across most of the U.S. The top five cold-weather cities are rounded out by Atlanta, Washington, D.C., St. Louis, and Cincinnati. The warm-weather top five includes San Diego, Austin, Dallas, and Phoenix.

**Whatever happened to Slip 'N Slide?** For a generation of kids, summer wasn't summer until someone unrolled that bright yellow streak of plastic stretched across a backyard and turned a garden hose into a carnival ride. Invented in 1961 by a California upholsterer named Robert Carrier (after he caught his son sliding on the wet driveway), the Slip 'N Slide became a suburban rite of passage. Through the 1970s and '80s, the Slip 'N Slide was everywhere. Wham-O, the toy company behind the Frisbee and Hula Hoop, sold millions. Kids raced side-by-side, daring each other to go faster, longer, or belly-flop harder. It was freedom and chaos in one gloriously wet afternoon. [You can see the TV ads here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDHO4yAQRk-DOyKYgEkKijTusu2WFoHBZoPBAuLIt1-xe4S_-aqneaP3-zk_TYghLb_Mhrr5PCf81oitYZlddguW-g3JhSOkZcvpjWctOAMDSTmnDGZnQjxnMT8Gpz0i4HVAzdUI7MbFnQ-rvtmX5FII40EgIKLyzKFUd6ukAiGGoPs9zhlwzoRkFzd6DpaLUaibktISwdCFlsvlTw6prejjmQ8sF5u3Ieq1tb2S64PARGBqK8bs26fuprSzIwSmLiAwsT5X9p-iNYY99XVIU24UD0z0ZUrBXoR2Ay3Zviu1-RMdrS3v9FMJTEPRbv-GGEMdFRFs2UyI_75pP4x4aPgbAAD__yZghr8). It became less fun in the 1990s, when adults and teens started showing up in hospital emergency rooms with spinal injuries. In 1993, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission issued a warning after several adults and one teenager suffered paralysis or neck injuries using Slip 'N Slides. Wham-O stopped promoting them for adults and began labeling slides clearly for kids under 12 only. From then on, Slip 'N Slides weren't really the same. They never disappeared--they just got a lot more restricted. You can still buy them, but they're usually shorter, safer, and softer, and often come with built-in inflatable bumpers or splash pools and with safety disclaimers printed everywhere. One final fun fact: the original Slip 'N Slide cost $8.95 in the 1960s. Today's deluxe "mega" versions with sprinklers and pools can run over $100--and still carry that "use at your own risk" fine print. _Did you own a Slip 'N Slide back in the day? Let us know your experience by replying to this email._

It’s in most dog foods and could be harming your pet’s health. . | Whatever happened to Slip 'N Slide? For a generation of kids, summer wasn't summer until someone unrolled that bright yellow streak of plastic stretched across a backyard and turned a garden hose into a carnival ride. Invented in 1961 by a California upholsterer named Robert Carrier (after he caught his son sliding on the wet driveway), the Slip 'N Slide became a suburban rite of passage. Through the 1970s and '80s, the Slip 'N Slide was everywhere. Wham-O, the toy company behind the Frisbee and Hula Hoop, sold millions. Kids raced side-by-side, daring each other to go faster, longer, or belly-flop harder. It was freedom and chaos in one gloriously wet afternoon. . It became less fun in the 1990s, when adults and teens started showing up in hospital emergency rooms with spinal injuries. In 1993, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission issued a warning after several adults and one teenager suffered paralysis or neck injuries using Slip 'N Slides. Wham-O stopped promoting them for adults and began labeling slides clearly for kids under 12 only. From then on, Slip 'N Slides weren't really the same. They never disappeared--they just got a lot more restricted. You can still buy them, but they're usually shorter, safer, and softer, and often come with built-in inflatable bumpers or splash pools and with safety disclaimers printed everywhere. One final fun fact: the original Slip 'N Slide cost $8.95 in the 1960s. Today's deluxe "mega" versions with sprinklers and pools can run over $100--and still carry that "use at your own risk" fine print. Did you own a Slip 'N Slide back in the day? | Peter,
We're huddled together this morning in Pennsylvania, waiting for the coffee to finish warming up. The air is cold, the wind is biting, and in just a few minutes we'll pour a cup, lace up our shoes, and head out the door.
Because we don't have the luxury of waiting. We've been out here for months, talking to voters about the unbelievable importance of this race. About how abortion rights, fair maps, and even the 2028 presidential election could be decided by the outcome of this single state Supreme Court retention vote.
---

name: Edge of fate
subject: Two days, two very different futures possible for America

body:
Losing this election means PA is lost to Democrats for at least a decade, and the Democrats path to reclaiming the White House would be near impossible.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PGiYpM0dcMVvxJxJQsayG1IFC8p9MmymaR6433wfwsnwwYcA0UcmZRxsnr1jjc317UWBnpXDlW1nX8AX_a85T34FSdKzd6gqPqj-a901avzKlQgLx9UiEDnvF_jvRqMNzEE1SC3JxTBYeFQsRNaR2sYY0ouAbXDDhs9ZiaT7tXUWJylE3ylai6luhMY-AsvLFLsffS59QFp6Hc0BZEmB46J0-65z_WPitgL2K7RywwY=&c=dW_zpOsWq-FOgD4OJ7toqONG3XUm1nRo02T74ZR8l3B60RK-pFesaA==&ch=L_qtb-1E1eXIjtsbLQzxPDVxd1qF7diw1oYQZFukebuR8XkUvf3cSQ==)

|
Peter,
In just two days, the polls will close for the most important election of 2025 for Democrats--the November 4th Pennsylvania Supreme Court retention election.
It is not exaggeration when we say this election is existential for Democrats. If MAGA wins this election, they will have control of the top court in the most important swing state in America, giving Republicans the power to gerrymander PA into a deep red state and use voter suppression to tilt the '26 and 28' elections in their favor.
Losing this election means PA is lost to Democrats for at least a decade, and the Democrats path to reclaiming the White House would be near impossible.
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PGiYpM0dcMVvxJxJQsayG1IFC8p9MmymaR6433wfwsnwwYcA0UcmZRxsnr1jjc317UWBnpXDlW1nX8AX_a85T34FSdKzd6gqPqj-a901avzKlQgLx9UiEDnvF_jvRqMNzEE1SC3JxTBYeFQsRNaR2sYY0ouAbXDDhs9ZiaT7tXUWJylE3ylai6luhMY-AsvLFLsffS59QFp6Hc0BZEmB45KmxmqOtl4Bo6ujJDaO_r0=&c=dW_zpOsWq-FOgD4OJ7toqONG3XUm1nRo02T74ZR8l3B60RK-pFesaA==&ch=L_qtb-1E1eXIjtsbLQzxPDVxd1qF7diw1oYQZFukebuR8XkUvf3cSQ==)
---
| Far-right activist Scott Pressler rallying MAGA to seize control of PA to prevent Democrats from taking back the White House.
What happens on Tuesday will alter the course of history:
1.) Democrats win and mount a comeback to reclaim the House in '26, the White House in '28, and put an end to the Trump era for good and begin to undo all the harm MAGA has done.
2.) Democrats lose, and allow MAGA to secure the White House for another decade, continuing their reign of terror and dismantling of our Democracy.
We will have the answer for the direction our nation will take in just two days -- 48 hours.
All publicly available data suggests this race is a dead heat, and it's coming down to a desperate race between Democrats and Republicans to turnout more voters than the other in the final stretch.
Defend Our Courts is throwing the kitchen sink at PA in these final 48 hours--TV ads, social media ads, canvassing--to try and ensure that the Democratic majority on the PA Supreme Court prevails and the better future is realized.
But the truth is, we don't know what the current state of the race is, and it's making us nervous. So we are redoubling our efforts today.
With your help, we can make an even bigger impact in these final 48 hours. Every dollar could be the difference right now. [Please, will you rush a contribution of $50, $25, or whatever you can spare to help defend this crucial court?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001PGiYpM0dcMVvxJxJQsayG1IFC8p9MmymaR6433wfwsnwwYcA0UcmZRxsnr1jjc317UWBnpXDlW1nX8AX_a85T34FSdKzd6gqPqj-a901avzKlQgLx9UiEDnvF_jvRqMNzEE1SC3JxTBYeFQsRNaR2sYY0ouAbXDDhs9ZiaT7tXUWJylE3ylai6luhMY-AsvLFLsffS59QFp6Hc0BZEmB400qCswJzfqP50pNDy6aPGU=&c=dW_zpOsWq-FOgD4OJ7toqONG3XUm1nRo02T74ZR8l3B60RK-pFesaA==&ch=L_qtb-1E1eXIjtsbLQzxPDVxd1qF7diw1oYQZFukebuR8XkUvf3cSQ==)

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

[Click here now to see the full story.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=2510866e03094b8aa9f6fbc37689129b&_e=AksDitCP6g5m11TL9mYjDYFIj_R-PTDrhYNsHsRvK8SxMYSwCvcJytVPD_a8PIonP2lv4vw-3pNV3cDHlFOWR7dK91UBVW_GS-JXiqgycQ9o4kSdyEncPDALw-ZVZcanC1u8VbK6lnhTVagHKgOszlpIdAQf1j2g6tBYd6uaJhdW3gHYtxYHs3XyWQ7L8Q2kWNj9dltaolf2XAmJPPuh7EOrOC5-JIhe34bPtjFJR87uZvNvifDL8Ci9QIQZuIx4O5RlU8-YDVXODFav9MMqeLNquUSgn9-pl9W7_t_G1628iYyJ5ahrkZ-LkjJbI5VNgyB4whIbeoo_sHuv-fWKr1oG3KjE2huTRFDeuVByd3LSfNozhGC2AXdgctZfTxwmq5MgtQGIsay5aNBnpvoa5DZwH-yukBI4plGi9Gp_7WvgltOAH35_yyQEs2OOgO_s2h7X_89HAVCGHLcFu7ybdRMaqEEb9vXgQwN0pNum_fQALiI2gkzCJgP93LpAkaoj-QVLfS7JvNNf38sJYt_HsU6I-hRpEqjgjyTz5Px5nXXe7bbKP1Z5Km5mgxH1e-xs)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

My fellow concerned American, I was one of the first savagely beaten by lawless, violent Antifa thugs. name: The Texas Flyover
subject: Goalposts Toppled, Newlyweds Return Home, and H-E-B Mystery Boxes
body:
The unranked SMU Mustangs scored a historic upset against No. 10 Miami yesterday, beating the Hurricanes 26-20 in overtime for the program’s first top-10 victory in 42 years. The improbable win likely knocks Miami out of playoff contention while keeping SMU’s hopes of reaching the ACC title game alive.

## Work Begins on STORM Ranch
Crews have begun clearing land for a 27-acre, $104 million swift-water training center near Houston, [expected to be the nation's largest](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2SnDAQhU8jMk1JPYyWCQicKFunDqlGakDlHomSepbl9i58hU1e9Op7P38-p09MnPL6G180yvdSpkxHYxKhOq0FeS9NmpR9Z4qZDj4Pim0CAw9trTYwRUx8Tn0Xx9nEp-1otB8OzNAP7tFtY3T9wz2HewgYe0fLjA9L2M-Lm_EOEbo0XjBrDVgw1sAtxsFZR_fBwuIAn6o3FJOUepONhL6xLXyWL6q3UF4dj5vI3tT9lwKvwB_HcQucwl_YyrtJyZdLgb9mKfBcArICf2Uq8NYo8HerwG9Ya2o6lHeWUx-JWc8Uyov0dokUnVFSyU0z1pWa6IVLibpSC2_SUjHllFe9YEic5FTguzrG_SKl5j5Ub9YXJv5fWn529dcI_wIAAP__99qVrA) when completed. Phase one will take about 18 to 22 months after construction begins early next year. STORM Ranch, a swift-water rescue training facility project of the Community Volunteer Fire Department and Harris Fort Bend Emergency Services District No. 100, will [provide a controlled environment](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSozAQRL9GZHJJA8g4UHAJ2V56ISWYEVatkChpbJa_v8K_sHG_6up-_76mLxdiSOtft5HlH5-nREeNxExlWrOLe65cOe97JEx0xPMgrBMo6KXWUsGELsRz6hq0s8KHbsjquwE1dIPpm6edzQNMfx9aUH7Ww7JAO2BrZtB-9miwCfYq01qBBqUV3BAHow21gwZvwD1EpwgD53LjJzH9uOrjmd9UbkvemmifzHsV7R8Bo4BxuZKQ1m_H5wUIGK9HAkafC8uZEn6obXulwKd85_hKTFSkD4Uk0u4Kb5RYzoXcd5Vrya-EMidpermFGAWMTbG4HyHGUM1ddGrdXIifOfw7iW8L_wMAAP__ba-JRQ) for first responders to prepare for catastrophic flooding events. The facility will include two swift-water channels, a pond for practicing boat rescues, and an urban village with buildings. The training center is expected to [accommodate 6,000 first responders](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OpDAQxfHTmMwtu_gwHTjYhGw23RAVuKBLMjaya4bh9queK0z8pKef_v8-5g_kyGn_iwd5-d7ynOiqkUSozHvGeOYqVfJ5RgqJrnhfFOoMBnptrTYwB-R4z10T_GLC0zbkrRvAjN049M3LEzrjlsH2A7Rg2-cS2rHvCMk9YV22vmH_PrPWgAVjDTxCGAc7UDta2AbAp-oMBZZcHvIioW-sW7zzF5XHmo8m-pfIWVX7R8GkYMJlte17UTC97QqmRJe-UKjoQnX9JC0FOXHa9YYrR5ZbV8nl0AXT-tLLJ0fRB9fKOemFUtBYCPVKSQpG_UPQW8w5cNoVTNYZ17WdUzA1xYfz4hi5Dk51Zj-Q449Tflf3y8P_AAAA__85VJCM) a year. "_The STORM Ranch will allow our teams to train in the same conditions they face during real-world incidents, ultimately saving lives_ ," said Cpt. Samantha Smith of the Community Volunteer Fire Department.
## Big Bend Getting 'Smart Wall'
Big Bend National Park will receive [new “Smart Wall” technology](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGuszAQBODTmA5kL8RA4eJv6PK3r0Q2uxBLDkb2Jg63fyJXeP3oG8383Oe79cHv23_7JMOfNc47lRyImdK8RRuOmDlzPI5AuFMJZyHMM0i41UrVEma0PpxzV6FxEkdVkVG9Bjl0g75VD9MurtMOsW3dOIJc1sGu1I8g1XKTC3aVNxemlAQFUkloEAetNLWDglWDHUUnCT3H1PCDmD42r-GMb0rNEp9VMA_mI4v2n4BJwFRKadb46e0rs9-viIDp2iRgcn6rHe1Yf5XaxYSU6kzLK3k-q2TwKD4En3UvOrk9rQ_fDv7bM28DvwEAAP__53x6CA) as part of the Trump administration’s $4.5 billion in federal contracts announced last month to enhance border security. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem designated the Big Bend Sector as “an area of high illegal entry” and waived contracting and environmental rules to expedite construction. Detection technology will be [installed along a 363-mile stretch](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAUAL8GOw08XcWC4hq7vfZKg_BwSZ5CgF3Wv7_c3SdcPclMMl_39a49-XP_1Aeq8nZhPbFmwlIwrXvQFEMuuYQYCe2Jla6KNq_A4dYK0XJYrfZ0rUNj1cbtLBpUYhqBy0GOt-ahpsls_TTP1vFBS-2Mlf3mpJjM6Exvbo1XPzIhOAjggkNnrRzFiL0U4EbQMxs4Wl9C6soDC751dnSFF6bOhKMh9SglZtZ_MFgYLLXWzmyx28OLwbKFZDG1Gc0z-XIxWDSFc2-fuf1DmcGSD51KWzVRe-jYJGVj9UQ-jxMb-H5oT7-p8r9BLwXfAQAA__9vWHyX) in the Big Bend region, part of about 535 miles of border monitored by cameras and sensors in areas deemed too remote or rugged for physical barriers. Most of the [funding for the contracts](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2unTAQhJ8GOiN7-T2FizR0N21KtNgLWFowsvce3_P2EXmFdCON9Gnm-_O1fGHgcO2_8SQrP1tcLiqZSYTSskfkO2bJEu-byV9U-FPI5wU09MoYpWHxGPizdLW3q_YvU5M14wB66qahrw_bbm6ErnV91w7kTAsTvhA00mjGyU1UB_vAjNFgQBsNjffTYAZqJwPbAPiqOk0-SEyNHCT0g3njT3xTalw8a7aHyJ2r9lcFcwVzKaVx693s8V3B_JxJMZ5PRAnxQlYn-YAqERNmqmD2R1ZuvRUWTF51_aouKsrFSxI6yer78pRUXNdV5ROTqILMT58lfbsHWifr7xKYQx7GqtP7iYH_rZP_c_q28DcAAP__T56O2Q) comes from the One Big Beautiful Bill that Congress passed this summer. Since 2021, 54 solar-powered Autonomous Surveillance Towers [equipped with artificial intelligence](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOgzAQRU9jOiN7IEAKF9vQZdstkfEMYMkwyHbicPtV9gpbf-nrvffzmB7WB3-s33Ynk98LTweVFChnitPKNpyccsp8noHwoBKuQpgmUHCTWksFE1ofrqmt0MwK77oio_sO1NAO3a3aTN_i3MyNWlxLrVU3t1BvYXG49L3F5l558znTWoEGpRXUiEOnO2oGDUsH9i5aRegzxzpvlOlt0xIuflGsHe9VMFvOZxLNl4BRwFhKqd181iu_BIwfmci8CxgDOxvkTuitjBTIJhIwzn6VMx0oE7nMUdL7tAcmufl1k5ncJmeOSPGzP6PPlzyoSPvMfPDOz1RFg2fxIfjU9aJV6259-APL_8v5MvAbAAD__wtDjo0) have been installed in the Big Bend Sector.
## SMU Topples Miami in Historic Upset
The unranked SMU Mustangs [scored a historic upset](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMGq2zAQRb9G3k2QZFt2Fl4EinevdFfoxoytcSyeZAlpEiV_X9Jf6O7ChcPh_P5avtB5d95_YqCJX3tcTqrFEzPl5R7Rp1i4cEzJkz2p-nclWxYtdQ9KgdSLReffS9fYaZX2qhqa1GC0HLvR9M0x9a1Vg0LbolGmX1s05rr2NGLbbt112Bo3fWBKSa20VFJfrB2NMtSOSu9G41V0kqzjmC98ENMLy-7f8Un5ssXQ-OlgTkW0N6Fnoeda6yWU8_MJPdMJjyL0XFLM_BmRD8pCzz0wfhNWfBfYcwxQwgOCw-AgPArjeS-QHt5D3HdwIeW44uoJqjvhmygBbhuwY09wxEQF0LsnCT1jhttN_frx-jM0ebKpOu9dMYPo5D2g8_-k-f9SPyf9NwAA___StpW2) against No. 10 Miami yesterday, beating the Hurricanes 26-20 in overtime for the program’s first top-10 victory in 42 years. The improbable win likely knocks Miami out of playoff contention while keeping SMU’s hopes of reaching the ACC title game alive. SMU tied the game with a 38-yard field goal with 25 seconds left in regulation. They elected to play defense first after winning the overtime coin toss. SMU safety Ahmaad Moses intercepted Miami quarterback Carson Beck on the ensuing possession, and the Mustangs [got the win](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3K4zAQAJ9G7mK0a_mvUHGN4Ypce6VZRWtHsLaEJaLk7Y_cK3z1wAzM3_t6pyDh3P_Qwba8t7ieXLNwKXyteyRJMZdcYkrC_uQqn8o-r6ixvwHcNK6egnxW03jrtJ-hYQvjgHoy09A3Tzt3w0Yd4-ycRxiNmwAnRHJAbiSDTbBfGYBGQA0aW--nAQbuJsBtQJqV0exDiVdbnlz4TXmTT3zx1T7i0Yh9lpKy6n4pXBQutdaWczq_UOHyiCK8822LsTgSUbhc_KCkcFkVLjsd_NsrXIyGsTf9BM1lfapBJORhVEbvBwX5Xyo_-_Oy-C8AAP__GA14mg) when running back T.J. Harden scored on a 1-yard run. After the victory, SMU fans rushed the field and [toppled one of the goalposts](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAQRE9jMpDdAwYHDjZx9jfdEDW4PWPJYIT7j4fbr1hpT_CTqqRUVXp_vuYvjCnuz9-4keVPyPNOtSRipnN-ZkxHLlw4H0civ1NNVyVfZpAwtEq1EmaPMV1z33i7SG9UQ1aNGuTUT3poXtaQX5ZBh9X0spdeBTKDGcKqDcGEo26ivcuUkqBAKgmd95NWmh6TgqABjegl-cj57PhFTB8sIV35TWe35q1J9sV8FPH4JcAJcLXW7rsgZ4_XHRDgCufzuv3IJxcBbl8RgwCH6yrA3fMCnFICnLylbN9tyJkXTKkNuJf2P4h2i7jFtsZdgJtG-TD9CFKOAlxzWn_UmFIsehS9fG4Y07-H_DOubwt_AwAA__8jFoqo), carrying it out of the stadium to the fountain at Dallas Hall. The celebration will likely cost SMU $50,000 for breaking the ACC’s newly updated event security policy, but university president Jay Hartzell didn’t seem too fazed. "_I neither condone nor support it, for the record, but I’ll pay the fine_ ," he [posted on social media](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFq8zAQwPGnkbcE3VmWpUHDxwemFNK1o1F850SgRMa6xnGfvqSv0PU__Ib_52k8xZTT_fIRbxzkOZfxzlvNLMLreCkxL6VKlbIsmenOW943pjqixu4AcNA4Ukx5H01D4azJQ8MBeovaGWe75hrOvaPJ93GirrPTZGJvz7ZttTeENJNvUnhhABoBNWg8EjkLllsHOFuMXhnNlKSsR7my8DPWOe_lwetxKrcmh6vIUlX7T-GgcHi-qsLh_f9bXOWbc1Y4VInyVRUO4J3pwVntOsQOdOuhWQMtW8o5Vdsroy-3mPIvLX8b8gj4EwAA___08XKH) shortly after the game.

The improbable win likely knocks Miami out of playoff contention while keeping SMU’s hopes of reaching the ACC title game alive. | | | | | | Sunday, November 2, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Work Begins on STORM Ranch Crews have begun clearing land for a 27-acre, $104 million swift-water training center near Houston, when completed. Phase one will take about 18 to 22 months after construction begins early next year. STORM Ranch, a swift-water rescue training facility project of the Community Volunteer Fire Department and Harris Fort Bend Emergency Services District No. 100, will for first responders to prepare for catastrophic flooding events. The facility will include two swift-water channels, a pond for practicing boat rescues, and an urban village with buildings. The training center is expected to a year. "The STORM Ranch will allow our teams to train in the same conditions they face during real-world incidents, ultimately saving lives ," said Cpt. Samantha Smith of the Community Volunteer Fire Department. Big Bend Getting 'Smart Wall' Big Bend National Park will receive as part of the Trump administration’s $4.5 billion in federal contracts announced last month to enhance border security. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem designated the Big Bend Sector as “an area of high illegal entry” and waived contracting and environmental rules to expedite construction. Detection technology will be in the Big Bend region, part of about 535 miles of border monitored by cameras and sensors in areas deemed too remote or rugged for physical barriers. Most of the comes from the One Big Beautiful Bill that Congress passed this summer. Since 2021, 54 solar-powered Autonomous Surveillance Towers have been installed in the Big Bend Sector. SMU Topples Miami in Historic Upset The unranked SMU Mustangs against No. 10 Miami yesterday, beating the Hurricanes 26-20 in overtime for the program’s first top-10 victory in 42 years. The improbable win likely knocks Miami out of playoff contention while keeping SMU’s hopes of reaching the ACC title game alive. SMU tied the game with a 38-yard field goal with 25 seconds left in regulation. They elected to play defense first after winning the overtime coin toss. SMU safety Ahmaad Moses intercepted Miami quarterback Carson Beck on the ensuing possession, and the Mustangs when running back T.J. Harden scored on a 1-yard run. After the victory, SMU fans rushed the field and , carrying it out of the stadium to the fountain at Dallas Hall. The celebration will likely cost SMU $50,000 for breaking the ACC’s newly updated event security policy, but university president Jay Hartzell didn’t seem too fazed. "I neither condone nor support it, for the record, but I’ll pay the fine ," he shortly after the game. | Flying together with our sponsor And when you sign up, you'll get access to our personalized deals newsletter—with curated picks from your favorite brands at the best stores. "It 's definitely a honeymoon we're not going to forget, but we're just really thankful and glad to be safe," Kasydee said. () ➤ Atascocita: An off-duty Harris County sheriff's deputy fatally shot a man who authorities said tried to enter the deputy's vehicle while his baby was in the back seat. The man's mother said he suffers from a schizoaffective disorder. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Elise Stefanik
subject: I went on Hannity to expose the truth
body:
New Yorkers are making their voices heard.

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bqIm/?__vbtrk=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
Friend, did you catch this interview?
I went on Sean Hannity’s show to expose the truth to the nation.

**WATCH THE CLIP:**

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bqIm/?__vbtrk=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**_Elise Stefanik on "The Sean Hannity Show"_**

Sean and I discussed how New Yorkers are FED UP with failed Democrat single party leadership!
They know that Kathy Hochul,**the WORST governor in America** , is destroying the Empire State from within by pushing policies that put violent criminals and illegals first while putting the needs of New Yorkers last.
Under her regime of sky-high taxes, open borders, and failed bail reform New York has become increasingly unaffordable and unsafe.
And just when we thought it couldn’t get any worse from New York Democrats,**Governor Hochul along with Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries have bent the knee** to the Far-Left of their party by**endorsing Zohran Mamdani** for Mayor of New York City.
Zohran Mamdani is a**radical communist** who unapologetically**promotes jihadists** and has called to both**DEFUND and DISMANTLE the police.**
This is who Kathy Hochul wants to run our nation’s largest city!
If we stand by and do nothing the Defund-The-Police Democrats led by Kathy Hochul and Zohran Mamdani will complete a full socialist takeover of New York from Albany to NYC.

[SAVE NEW YORK](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bqIm/?__vbtrk=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Fellow patriot, I am not giving up on New York, this is my home and I know that we cannot Make America Great Again until we save our nation’s states and cities from the grip of the Radical Left.
And I am not alone. The polls are continuing to shift in our favor with recent independent polls indicating that I am LEADING Kathy Hochul. **This race is now a TOSS-UP!**

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bqIm/?__vbtrk=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**_Independent Poll Shows Elise Stefanik LEADING Kathy Hochul in the NY Governor Race, Giving Hope to millions of New Yorkers Terrified of a Mamdani Communist Takeover_**

We are gaining momentum and poised to flip NY in 2026!
This state is now in play because New York Democrats stand with Mamdani while I stand with New Yorkers who want safe streets, affordable communities, and real leadership.
I’ve been the loudest, strongest, and most consistent fighter to **SAVE NEW YORK** and **_[with your help I will continue to FIGHT BACK against Mamdani and FIRE HOCHUL once and for all](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bqIm/?__vbtrk=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**

[STAND WITH ELISE](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bqIm/?__vbtrk=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
There’s no hiding from this fight. Kathy Hochul has decided to make Zohran Mamdani the new face of the Democrat Party.
If we don’t hold the line here their radical platform will become the model for the next wave of Democrats across the country.
But with your continued support we can deliver a victory in New York next year that will replace Hochul with America First leadership and secure several battleground House seats that are crucial to preserving the GOP House Majority.
I am counting on you to help us seize this moment.

_Your fellow patriot,_
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bqIm/?__vbtrk=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**Elise Stefanik**
Proud New Yorker
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/bqIm/?__vbtrk=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzMwNTYzOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Be a part of history
body:
You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace.

| | |
---
| | Dear Friend, Please -- for the sake of all our families -- take it from me as someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life... You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children. You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace. But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on _Blue's Clues_...
---
[](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyMkb2O2zoQhZ-G6iiQwz-pUGGtLOMWvghcOEglUOTIJiKJBkk7yNsHXmT7lPMNZoDvnO_n6WzDGvbb_3bDzs1TytOM9mFTics95BLT7wkYKMo5ZfBe88p3rkWQs6yw40YDa5QRprp3KBzM0nLdsEV72RhQxslGN1yIVqqlCt37F-cMOHAlVO1n4_1iXLtIrY1VRDL0ocRUu7hnTC9bwgvnFK13Npd6x1Kt3b2URybiQGAkMGZ0z4S13dHHUru4ERjnZF9I5xh_ZgKjAWhdwyXV0gOVChVtvQfqF5yd14rJ1hIxPss25fhMDokY-uFEL8crAf3GG_rw3IgYjue_xNntYcNtJ2L4dGJMTP1wmvrL4Xqc_judfoyf8-V4nb4d6Ef_dRf3gnshYvjo6VcaVer841dY15C1IZLdNhvWt0pV_rWUVwd_AgAA__8CoY-n)

| | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses. And the result is an upside-down world where children lecture YOU about what you can and cannot say... ...and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas. Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. [**Today, I'm inviting you to support the very first children 's television show that actively fights back against the groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical values that made America great.**](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyMkb2O2zoQhZ-G6iiQwz-pUGGtLOMWvghcOEglUOTIJiKJBkk7yNsHXmT7lPMNZoDvnO_n6WzDGvbb_3bDzs1TytOM9mFTics95BLT7wkYKMo5ZfBe88p3rkWQs6yw40YDa5QRprp3KBzM0nLdsEV72RhQxslGN1yIVqqlCt37F-cMOHAlVO1n4_1iXLtIrY1VRDL0ocRUu7hnTC9bwgvnFK13Npd6x1Kt3b2URybiQGAkMGZ0z4S13dHHUru4ERjnZF9I5xh_ZgKjAWhdwyXV0gOVChVtvQfqF5yd14rJ1hIxPss25fhMDokY-uFEL8crAf3GG_rw3IgYjue_xNntYcNtJ2L4dGJMTP1wmvrL4Xqc_judfoyf8-V4nb4d6Ef_dRf3gnshYvjo6VcaVer841dY15C1IZLdNhvWt0pV_rWUVwd_AgAA__8CoY-n) And it's only possible with the help of grandparents and seniors like you. Introducing:_ Iggy and Mr. Kirk_. This ground-breaking live-action television show captivates children... ...but more importantly, it builds their character at the same time. Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of the truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. **[Because I can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help.](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJyMkb2O2zoQhZ-G6iiQwz-pUGGtLOMWvghcOEglUOTIJiKJBkk7yNsHXmT7lPMNZoDvnO_n6WzDGvbb_3bDzs1TytOM9mFTics95BLT7wkYKMo5ZfBe88p3rkWQs6yw40YDa5QRprp3KBzM0nLdsEV72RhQxslGN1yIVqqlCt37F-cMOHAlVO1n4_1iXLtIrY1VRDL0ocRUu7hnTC9bwgvnFK13Npd6x1Kt3b2URybiQGAkMGZ0z4S13dHHUru4ERjnZF9I5xh_ZgKjAWhdwyXV0gOVChVtvQfqF5yd14rJ1hIxPss25fhMDokY-uFEL8crAf3GG_rw3IgYjue_xNntYcNtJ2L4dGJMTP1wmvrL4Xqc_judfoyf8-V4nb4d6Ef_dRf3gnshYvjo6VcaVer841dY15C1IZLdNhvWt0pV_rWUVwd_AgAA__8CoY-n)** Millions of families are already watching Season One of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ on our streaming service BRAVE+, YouTube, and on DVD. And the reviews from parents, grandparents, and kids are incredible. One Mom of three little girls told us: "Our family was struggling through a pretty difficult day. But I turned on _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ and that was exactly what my heart needed as much as theirs." It's amazing that your simple response to this letter can make that kind of difference for a family. Each episode costs around $70,000 to produce. I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously we won't work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace. But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on Blue's Clues... | | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses. body:
Losing this election means PA is lost to Democrats for at least a decade, and the Democrats path to reclaiming the White House would be near impossible.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vIMU6wSzX7Rt3hn3NQ8cqSXaDH95hqdL5Aqu0Y_URdOLAGXgSdn4a_G0qtOFRnAqXzjnu3t8Jv4hAmiZJHkBMFsHGR40OneahuTXnCTUInHOPr3b1k_vIXooYLREdkl5Ve75x2XwAqdM1uCLa7q91tkb0zjANumt4y1h4L50wZP9tHkqGY2xhI20euaesg3JN4tR-dVBeZhF3g4ZxmtdsrgnD_oGneBMhHTiKBPNpm8=&c=zg4H2N01LppHJUYTCckilQAwwynDSQb3XRbt9bsIc2AzioY5hL3u3A==&ch=yJaAPmn9MEvqUa_opakjjWr-Y0sWLVB-VwK3u2qlrPvWJrLDnu2I4g==)
---
|
Peter,
In just two days, the polls will close for the most important election of 2025 for Democrats--the November 4th Pennsylvania Supreme Court retention election.
It is not exaggeration when we say this election is existential for Democrats. If MAGA wins this election, they will have control of the top court in the most important swing state in America, giving Republicans the power to gerrymander PA into a deep red state and use voter suppression to tilt the '26 and 28' elections in their favor.
Losing this election means PA is lost to Democrats for at least a decade, and the Democrats path to reclaiming the White House would be near impossible.
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vIMU6wSzX7Rt3hn3NQ8cqSXaDH95hqdL5Aqu0Y_URdOLAGXgSdn4a_G0qtOFRnAqXzjnu3t8Jv4hAmiZJHkBMFsHGR40OneahuTXnCTUInHOPr3b1k_vIXooYLREdkl5Ve75x2XwAqdM1uCLa7q91tkb0zjANumt4y1h4L50wZP9tHkqGY2xhI20euaesg3JN4tR-dVBeZhF3g4ZxmtdsuUixvQj5q064khmbuU26hg=&c=zg4H2N01LppHJUYTCckilQAwwynDSQb3XRbt9bsIc2AzioY5hL3u3A==&ch=yJaAPmn9MEvqUa_opakjjWr-Y0sWLVB-VwK3u2qlrPvWJrLDnu2I4g==)

| Far-right activist Scott Pressler rallying MAGA to seize control of PA to prevent Democrats from taking back the White House.
What happens on Tuesday will alter the course of history:
1.) Democrats win and mount a comeback to reclaim the House in '26, the White House in '28, and put an end to the Trump era for good and begin to undo all the harm MAGA has done.
2.) Democrats lose, and allow MAGA to secure the White House for another decade, continuing their reign of terror and dismantling of our Democracy.
We will have the answer for the direction our nation will take in just two days -- 48 hours.
All publicly available data suggests this race is a dead heat, and it's coming down to a desperate race between Democrats and Republicans to turnout more voters than the other in the final stretch.
Defend Our Courts is throwing the kitchen sink at PA in these final 48 hours--TV ads, social media ads, canvassing--to try and ensure that the Democratic majority on the PA Supreme Court prevails and the better future is realized.
But the truth is, we don't know what the current state of the race is, and it's making us nervous. So we are redoubling our efforts today.
With your help, we can make an even bigger impact in these final 48 hours. Every dollar could be the difference right now. clean_body: Losing this election means PA is lost to Democrats for at least a decade, and the Democrats path to reclaiming the White House would be near impossible. | | | | | == | Peter, In just two days, the polls will close for the most important election of 2025 for Democrats--the November 4th Pennsylvania Supreme Court retention election. It is not exaggeration when we say this election is existential for Democrats. If MAGA wins this election, they will have control of the top court in the most important swing state in America, giving Republicans the power to gerrymander PA into a deep red state and use voter suppression to tilt the '26 and 28' elections in their favor. Losing this election means PA is lost to Democrats for at least a decade, and the Democrats path to reclaiming the White House would be near impossible. | | == | Far-right activist Scott Pressler rallying MAGA to seize control of PA to prevent Democrats from taking back the White House. What happens on Tuesday will alter the course of history: 1.) Democrats win and mount a comeback to reclaim the House in '26, the White House in '28, and put an end to the Trump era for good and begin to undo all the harm MAGA has done. 2.) Democrats lose, and allow MAGA to secure the White House for another decade, continuing their reign of terror and dismantling of our Democracy. We will have the answer for the direction our nation will take in just two days -- 48 hours. All publicly available data suggests this race is a dead heat, and it's coming down to a desperate race between Democrats and Republicans to turnout more voters than the other in the final stretch. Defend Our Courts is throwing the kitchen sink at PA in these final 48 hours--TV ads, social media ads, canvassing--to try and ensure that the Democratic majority on the PA Supreme Court prevails and the better future is realized. But the truth is, we don't know what the current state of the race is, and it's making us nervous. So we are redoubling our efforts today. With your help, we can make an even bigger impact in these final 48 hours. Every dollar could be the difference right now. == | | | | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | 2 days. 2 wildly different futures. What we do in these next 48 hours will help determine the fate of our nation for the next decade. It's now or never. This is probably the cheapest survival food I have found, and the company is great."_ _**Gary M.**

I am going to stock up again as soon as I settle into my new job. Everyone should participate with this company. You will not be disappointed."_ _**John H.**
"We're in Florida and have made many preparations for the aftermath of a possible hurricane. You can see the chunks of potatoes and carrots in each bite you take. It's a good soup." Kevin S. "Perfect starter kit for any scenario. Put one in your car, work bag, in your house or RV. If you want to start preparing safe food, this is where to start." Carol B. "I have been iced in this weekend. I thought I would try my potato soup. To my utter delight, IT WAS DELICIOUS. Thank you for such a wonderful product and the peace of mind it brings." Ken K. "I am not a full-blown survivalist. I am not an idiot, either. I have been through enough in my life and have seen friends who have been through an emergency situation. Save up if you have to, but get at least a month's supply. It tastes good. Although if it's a dire of an emergency, you will be happy to eat anything. IF you have something to eat for your family. We made some of this product, and had family and friends over for them to taste. They all agreed they didn't think anything would be this good, and they will be ordering. Just do something. You can't miss out on this deal." Justin A. "My wife and I tried the food and we were both surprised about how good it tasted and how satisfied it made us feel afterwards. It feels good knowing that I can provide for my family if a crisis arose and I intend to get more in the future. Also the shipping and customer service has been top notch. This is probably the cheapest survival food I have found, and the company is great." Gary M. "I actually had lost my job and was homeless for a while. I dug into my food supply, and I cannot fully describe how delicious and easy to prepare everything is. I felt like I was eating like a king. I am going to stock up again as soon as I settle into my new job. Everyone should participate with this company. You will not be disappointed." John H. "We're in Florida and have made many preparations for the aftermath of a possible hurricane. While we are thankful that Florida has not been hit in several years, it gives us great peace of mind to know we have our 4Patriots kit stashed away, knowing it's not a case of "if" but "when"."_

subject: These people will swamp Dems unless we act now
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
Step 1 for MAGA’s unconstitutional plans to install Trump as president for life is to knock out the Dem majority on this court.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UySJ2Plvnt3_DgyAbgLYqfe9_37WVApPTk7CgSuiIn1Bg1rlnkiAoFGbQcp7wW-NSUEv_55x5u9QLT7Wc35YFQ4fek-7yYB_nKg7sETwviguYgTUNErQ6Rk4KHBk7wzGk88I6BpsGRFTSBZhHul-W13HYPldVh2GS6Lj3m6VfDerrqc5TsHjNr3Jms2LFwJHWq6xUI0KuK0OS1Ud9ShbiXDKaf9wTzO9JfWn5cYSG6o=&c=_VJWj3W1NTpFOt18Ej3KSFcGiSTHm4jL8Z4ad-xfkq0xXwdj7JRfgw==&ch=vp4sZJBiUIFNOrzNlR8b6OLPHrfGDIoqpbgwvLtxfFYGrJAzduuY7g==)
---
|
Peter,
Our canvassers have been going door to door for months now, meeting every voter they can--both Republican and Democrat--and explaining the super high stakes of the upcoming PA election on Tuesday.
The 5-2 liberal majority of the Pennsylvania state Supreme Court is on the line this November 4th. Three Dem justices are up for retention--or defeat--in a race that will serve as a bellwether for 2026.
Trump is watching. MAGA is watching. Dangerous far-right lunatics like Steve Bannon, who is openly plotting how to keep Trump in power past 2028, are watching.
Step 1 for MAGA's unconstitutional plans to install Trump as president for life is to knock out the Dem majority on this court.
That's why our message has been well-received by almost everyone we've reached. Well, almost everyone. Of course, there are always people like this guy Thomas who blindly supports Trump and vote however MAGA wants him to:
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UySJ2Plvnt3_DgyAbgLYqfe9_37WVApPTk7CgSuiIn1Bg1rlnkiAoFGbQcp7wW-NSUEv_55x5u9QLT7Wc35YFQ4fek-7yYB_nKg7sETwviguYgTUNErQ6Rk4KHBk7wzGk88I6BpsGRFTSBZhHul-W13HYPldVh2GS6Lj3m6VfDerrqc5TsHjNr3Jms2LFwJHWq6xUI0KuK0OS1Ud9ShbidJeexJXVZFiIW6ZTxPqDqg=&c=_VJWj3W1NTpFOt18Ej3KSFcGiSTHm4jL8Z4ad-xfkq0xXwdj7JRfgw==&ch=vp4sZJBiUIFNOrzNlR8b6OLPHrfGDIoqpbgwvLtxfFYGrJAzduuY7g==)
---
| The good news is, for every Tom there are two Pennsylvania voters who will vote Yes--but only if we can reach them in time.
With Election Day less than 3 days away, our window for turning out every possible 'Yes' voter is rapidly closing.
The dorky MAGA "Tom" vote could end up swamping us if we let up now. We have approximately 48 hours to make it otherwise.
[Will you rush a last-minute donation of $55 (the average cost of earning a Yes vote in the 2025 PA election) or whatever you can spare to neutralize the votes of people like Tom? Democrats should be celebrating this huge win the day after Election Day--but we are currently falling behind and need to catch up in these final hours:](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001UySJ2Plvnt3_DgyAbgLYqfe9_37WVApPTk7CgSuiIn1Bg1rlnkiAoFGbQcp7wW-NSUEv_55x5u9QLT7Wc35YFQ4fek-7yYB_nKg7sETwviguYgTUNErQ6Rk4KHBk7wzGk88I6BpsGRFTSBZhHul-W13HYPldVh2GS6Lj3m6VfDerrqc5TsHjNr3Jms2LFwJHWq6xUI0KuK0OS1Ud9Shbickh8WtwGvKDS7_fICq0bbo=&c=_VJWj3W1NTpFOt18Ej3KSFcGiSTHm4jL8Z4ad-xfkq0xXwdj7JRfgw==&ch=vp4sZJBiUIFNOrzNlR8b6OLPHrfGDIoqpbgwvLtxfFYGrJAzduuY7g==)

clean_body: Step 1 for MAGA’s unconstitutional plans to install Trump as president for life is to knock out the Dem majority on this court. | | | | | == | Peter, Our canvassers have been going door to door for months now, meeting every voter they can--both Republican and Democrat--and explaining the super high stakes of the upcoming PA election on Tuesday. The 5-2 liberal majority of the Pennsylvania state Supreme Court is on the line this November 4th. Three Dem justices are up for retention--or defeat--in a race that will serve as a bellwether for 2026. Trump is watching. MAGA is watching. Dangerous far-right lunatics like Steve Bannon, who is openly plotting how to keep Trump in power past 2028, are watching. Step 1 for MAGA's unconstitutional plans to install Trump as president for life is to knock out the Dem majority on this court. That's why our message has been well-received by almost everyone we've reached. Well, almost everyone. Of course, there are always people like this guy Thomas who blindly supports Trump and vote however MAGA wants him to: | | == | The good news is, for every Tom there are two Pennsylvania voters who will vote Yes--but only if we can reach them in time. With Election Day less than 3 days away, our window for turning out every possible 'Yes' voter is rapidly closing. The dorky MAGA "Tom" vote could end up swamping us if we let up now. We have approximately 48 hours to make it otherwise. == | | | | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | == | 48 hours to go until day zero. name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: Delco hides budget before election day
body:
News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | |
---
| | | Good morning, and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Weekly Reads.

Election Day 2025 draws near! To stay up to date on late-breaking stories and post-election analysis, [subscribe to our daily email list](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=74df7e5135&e=b7b3eddce2) and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
---
|
### **1.[Delaware County conceals budget details for year, refuses transparency before election](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=5b796b91ff&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Todd Shepherd_
Delaware County and its five members of the county council are continuing a year-long campaign of concealing elements of the budget and the budget process from the view of citizens and the media in the wake of last year’s 23.7 percent tax increase that sparked widespread outcry.

Most importantly to those who will vote in a week, the county is being coy about whether it will publicly produce the first draft of the budget before Election Day, a move that would deny voters an understanding of just how large the next tax increase could be. One council member has already conceded taxes will increase for the third straight year.

**Why It Matters**. For calendar year 2024, the council raised taxes by five percent. For 2025, it raised taxes 24 percent. In 2026, taxes will likely go up again, the only question is: by how much?

It’s not a question voters will have the answer to before going to the polls. That’s a big problem.
Voters have to choose their representatives based on something — past performance, future promises, party platforms — and providing the voter with more information makes for a well-informed electorate.

When the Democrats on county council refuse to give the voters information on the most important issue in their power — the county budget — they’re weakening the democratic process and depriving the voters of the tools they need to make an informed choice.

[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=b05174bb36&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
### **2.[Pandemic policies sparked a transgender spike — and the reversal has begun](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=18c4fe881a&e=b7b3eddce2)**

_By Beth Ann Rosica_
According to a recent study, transgender social contagion appears to be on the decline with numbers closer to pre-pandemic levels. For those of us who fought against extended school closures and lockdowns, this data comes as no surprise. The Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE) in partnership with College Pulse conducts an annual[ survey](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=0444a536fb&e=b7b3eddce2) of college students to rank schools based on free speech. The study includes questions related to gender and sexual orientation, and historical results show a peak in 2023 for students who identify as gender non-conforming and those who list their sexual orientation as bi-sexual, queer, questioning and other. **Why It Matters.** In 2023 on the third anniversary of school closures, I[ wrote](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a73733e05f&e=b7b3eddce2) about the exponential rise of gender ideology in K-12 schools. At the time, I spoke with a local school psychologist who asked to remain anonymous, and he told me that over the course of 20 years prior to Covid, he would see one to two students who identified differently from their biological sex. When returning to the classroom post-Covid, that number rose exponentially to about 20 to 25 percent of all kids, and now has come back down to closer to five percent. His assertions in 2023 are consistent with the FIRE data, as less than four percent of college students identified as gender non-conforming in 2025. For those who doubted the extended school closures and lockdowns did not contribute or cause the significant rise of transgenderism, the data is clear. Children and young people suffered greatly as the result of draconian policies and political maneuvering by groups like the national teachers’ unions. Adolescent mental health plummeted during this time, and several studies have shown a strong correlation between gender dysphoria and other mental health issues. **[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=0281f0d3c0&e=b7b3eddce2)**
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* **[Christine Flowers: Blame for violence continuing in Philadelphia lies at DA’s doorstep](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e927730f68&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Martina White: Larry Krasner’s masked hypocrisy](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=3221bc7a3b&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Guy Ciarrocchi: Most families already have school choice. What about the rest?](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=0855f9a757&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Paul Davis: The thorn in Krasner’s side — my interview with Ralph Cipriano](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=16a75104e2&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Ben Mannes: How Philly’s justice system failed Ellen Greenberg](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=75976af906&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Frank Ryan: Pennsylvania and New Jersey’s race to the bottom](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=fb35fec010&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Thom Nickels: Harvard’s atheist chaplain](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=fa8d8ad2d7&e=b7b3eddce2)**

---
|
---
|
# **4\. What we're reading**
At _Compact_ last week, Helen Andrews wrote an essay that caused some considerable stir around the internet. In “[The Great Feminization](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c696cbce2e&e=b7b3eddce2),” Andrews describes the rise of the woke movement as linked inextricably with the rise of female empowerment in the American workplace.

It’s a bold take, one that was met with both agreement and disagreement elsewhere — see, for example, [Matt Yglesias’s take](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c60681b1f6&e=b7b3eddce2) on the essay, or [Tiana Lowe Doescher’s](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ad8ed53319&e=b7b3eddce2) at the _Washington Examiner_. Reasonable minds may disagree — they certainly did at our office — but it’s all worth reading and being a part of the conversation.****
---
|
---
| [ ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=38daea3a1d&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
| **_Broad + Liberty_ is funded by readers like you.**

Your generous support ensures that _Broad + Liberty_ can bring you stories and opinions that Pennsylvania’s mainstream media would rather leave unheard. Please consider making a contribution today. If you would like your gift to be used to help us cover a specific story or subject, please let us know in the contribution form or at [editors@broadandliberty.com](mailto:editirs@broadandliberty.com).

As always, gifts made to _Broad + Liberty_ are 100% tax deductible!

Thank you, dear reader, for your steadfast support of our independent, broad-minded brand of local journalism. We could not do this without you.

With gratitude,

— The Editors at _Broad + Liberty_

---
